hypothesis: Enhancing SSTR2 selectivity by optimizing aromatic complementarity at position 5 with ECL2 residues (SSTR2-unique 192/193/195/197) and introducing steric differentiation at position 11 to avoid off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 binding.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy emphasizes ddG binding energy but fails to address off-target interactions. A mutation-driven approach targeting non-pharmacophore positions is necessary to achieve selectivity over SSTR3/SSTR5.

proposed_changes.rationale: To disrupt conserved pocket interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 at non-pharmacophore positions while preserving key pharmacophore residues (7-10 and Cys3/Cys14).
These mutations are designed to break conserved interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5 binding pockets without affecting the pharmacophore region.
